content_type: Educational presentation - MIS curriculum research study
conference: SAGES 2018 - Society of American Gastrointestinal and Endoscopic Surgeons
study_focus: Proficiency-based FES curriculum implementation for general surgery residents
reason_not_scored: This is a research presentation/educational lecture about an FES training curriculum study, not a recorded performance of an FLS task. It contains slides with study methodology, results, and conclusions, but no video of an actual trainee or expert performing any of the five FLS manual tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture).

content_summary.study_design: Proficiency-based training curriculum for general surgery residents focusing on cognitive skills (FES didactics) and manual skills (simulator training to expert level benchmarks)
content_summary.participants: 28 general surgery residents from two programs; 25 completed curriculum (89%)
key_findings: Significant improvements in GAGES gastroscopy (p<0.001) and colonoscopy (p<0.001) scores post-training
FES manual exam pass rate: 84% (vs 68% in previous report)
Mucosal inspection skill correlates with task completion time and FES pass/fail outcome
Simulator-generated scores did not correlate with FES manual exam performance
content_summary.conclusion: Proficiency-based endoscopic skills curriculum successfully improved resident competency and FES exam pass rates

educational_topics_covered: Assessment tools for endoscopic and manual surgical skills
Correlation between simulator performance and real-world clinical assessment

confidence_rationale: This is clearly an educational/research presentation with title slides, study design diagrams, data tables, and statistical results. No FLS task performance is shown.
